title: xiaoyi-ssg Template Manifest Schema
description: A site is described by two orthogonal, open abstractions: `sources` (where data comes from) and `views` (how pages are generated). The engine treats every source uniformly regardless of adapter type. See prompts/template-manifest-generation.md.

sources.description: Named data sources. Each key is a source name; each value is a source definition resolved by a Source Adapter (markdown / http / json / csv / rss / inline / derived). See prompts/data-sources.md.

dir.description: type=markdown: content directory relative to site root

url.description: type=http|json|csv|rss: absolute URL fetched at BUILD TIME only

file.description: type=json|csv: local file path relative to site root

body.description: type=http POST: request body (object → JSON-encoded, string → raw)

auth.description: Secret injection. Only the env var NAME is stored; the value is read from process.env at build time and never written to public/ or snapshots.

env.description: Environment variable name holding the secret

select.description: JSONPath-lite expression selecting the item array from the response

from.description: type=derived: name of the source this one is computed from

op.description: type=derived: transform applied to the `from` source

field.description: type=derived: field the op operates on

map.description: Field remapping into the standard item shape

defaults.description: Default values merged under each item when a mapped/normalized field is absent

tree.description: Build a tree structure from parent/nav_order fields

cache.description: Build-time snapshot cache for remote sources (http/json/csv/rss). Snapshots are written to .xiaoyi-ssg/.cache/sources/<key>.json (git-ignored).

ttl.description: Seconds the snapshot is considered fresh; 0 = always refetch

fallback.description: Behavior when a remote fetch fails

views.description: View nodes. Each view is either a layout (type=layout) or a page generator (type=page). Page views expand via `for`.

layout.description: Name of a layout view (required when type=page)

for.description: Expansion strategy. Omit `for` entirely for a single page (home, 404, computed).

each.description: Source name. One output page per item.

paginate.description: Source name. One output page per pagination slice.

perPage.description: Items per page. Required when `paginate` is set.

use.description: Source names whose full datasets are injected as top-level variables (multi-source aggregation)

lang.description: If set, expand once per language code (adds {lang} placeholder)

globals.description: Global data available to every view
